title: Seed-domain selection (scored vs. criteria; low-risk, source-rich) — gates later cycles
context: ATLTUAE is a self-improving, perpetually-looping open research+analysis engine whose deliverable is an ever-improving fully-cited synthesis plus a public coverage ledger - never a premature final answer. Early cycles must be tractable and license-clean, so the engine needs one well-chosen seed domain before any cycle runs. This M0 research task scores candidate seed domains against explicit criteria so the first bounded cycle is source-rich, low-risk, and has a tractable taxonomy. No partner, steward, or credentialed expert reviewer is yet secured.
objective: Produce a scored seed-domain selection memo that picks one starting domain for the first bounded cycle, evaluated against explicit criteria (source-rich, low-risk, tractable taxonomy, clear public value, open/PD/CC source availability), with the rationale and runner-up alternatives recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the scoring criteria (source-rich, low-risk per the domain-risk policy, tractable taxonomy, public value, availability of open/PD/CC sources) and scores at least three candidate domains against them
Selects one seed domain with recorded rationale, and lists runner-up alternatives that can be promoted later
Confirms the selected domain is NOT health/legal/safety/financial high-risk (or, if it touches them, routes those surfaces through the 'not advice' + expert-review policy)
Confirms sufficient open/PD/CC license-clean source availability for at least one bounded cycle on the chosen domain
